title: dryopria
intro: Dryopria, also known as Dryopteris, is a genus of ferns, commonly referred to as male ferns, which are native to the Northern Hemisphere. The medicinal properties of the dryopria plant include its use in treating parasitic worm infections, particularly tapeworms, due to its anthelmintic properties. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support liver function. Dryopria is often prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of tea, tincture, or capsules, and is also used in traditional medicine for its purgative and emetic properties.
